info.title: Notabene API
info.description: API for managing entities, relationships, and transactions in the Notabene system.

get.summary: List customers for an entity within Flow workflows
get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of customers associated with an entity within Flow
workflow contexts. Customer PII in profileData is encrypted and not included
by default for security and privacy compliance.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of customers to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of customers to skip for pagination

entityDid.description: Entity DID that owns this customer

profileData.description: Encrypted IVMS101 customer profile data (PII encrypted)

post.summary: Create a new customer for Flow workflows
post.description:
Creates a new customer profile within a Flow workflow context. Customer PII is
encrypted before storage and returned without PII by default. This endpoint
checks for existing customers to prevent duplicates.

**IVMS101 Validation Requirements:**
- For natural persons: `name` object is **required**
- For legal persons: `name` and `nationalIdentification` objects are **required**
- When `nameIdentifier` array is provided, its fields are **required**
- Unknown fields are rejected (strict validation)

customerDid.description: Customer identifier (mailto:, tel:, or did:web/did:ethr/did:pkh/did:key/did:jwk/did:keri). When a DID is used, profileData.contactInformation.email is required for OTP.

customerType.description: Type of customer (natural person or legal entity)

nameIdentifier.description: Array of name identifiers (optional, but fields required when present)

primaryIdentifier.description: Primary name (e.g., surname) - REQUIRED when nameIdentifier present

naturalPersonNameIdentifierType.description: Type of name identifier (e.g., LEGL) - REQUIRED when nameIdentifier present

nameIdentifier.description: Array of name identifiers (optional, but fields required when present)

legalPersonName.description: Legal entity name - REQUIRED when nameIdentifier present

legalPersonNameIdentifierType.description: Type of name identifier - REQUIRED when nameIdentifier present

nationalIdentifier.description: National identifier value (e.g., registration number) - REQUIRED

entityDid.description: Entity DID that owns this customer

profileData.description: Encrypted IVMS101 customer profile data (PII encrypted)

get.summary: Get a customer profile by DID
get.description:
Retrieves a customer profile within a Flow workflow context. Customer PII can be
optionally included in the response based on the includePII query parameter and
user permissions. PII data is decrypted on-demand when requested.

parameters.name: customerDID
parameters.description: Customer identifier (mailto:, tel:, or supported DID method)

parameters.name: includePII
parameters.description: Whether to decrypt and include PII data in profileData

entityDid.description: Entity DID that owns this customer

profileData.description:
IVMS101 customer profile data. If includePII=false (default), PII is encrypted.
If includePII=true, PII is decrypted and returned in plaintext.

put.summary: Update a customer profile within Flow workflows
put.description:
Updates an existing customer profile within a Flow workflow context. Customer PII
is automatically encrypted before storage when profile data is updated. Only
specified fields are updated, maintaining data integrity.

**IVMS101 Validation Requirements (when profileData is provided):**
- Same validation rules as POST apply when updating profileData
- For natural persons: `name` object is **required**
- For legal persons: `name` and `nationalIdentification` objects are **required**
- When `nameIdentifier` array is provided, its fields are **required**
- Unknown fields are rejected (strict validation)

parameters.name: customerDID
parameters.description: Customer identifier to update (mailto:, tel:, or supported DID method)

customerType.description: Type of customer (must match profileData if provided)

profileData.description:
IVMS101-compliant customer profile data (will be encrypted).
When provided, must follow same structure as POST endpoint with required fields.
See POST /entities/{entityDID}/flow/customers for full schema.

delete.summary: Delete a customer profile within Flow workflows
delete.description:
Permanently removes a customer profile from a Flow workflow context. This action
cannot be undone and will remove all associated customer data and PII.

parameters.name: customerDID
parameters.description: Customer DID identifier to delete

post.summary: Create a new payin for a customer within Flow workflows
post.description:
Creates a new payin transaction for a customer within a Flow workflow context.
This endpoint initiates a payment workflow where the customer (merchant) can
receive payments. The payin includes invoice data, supported assets, and
generates a payment link for customer interaction.

parameters.name: customerDID
parameters.description: Customer DID identifier (this is the merchant/beneficiary in payin context)

currency.description: Currency code for the transaction (e.g., USD, EUR)

supportedAssets.description: List of supported assets for this transaction (CAIP-19 format, or fiat codes)

fallbackSettlementAddresses.description: List of fallback settlement addresses (CAIP-10 or payto://) for the merchant

invoice.description: Invoice details for the transaction (optional, complex invoices require customer approval)

lineTotal.description: Total price for this line item

400.description: Invalid request data or validation error

409.description: Payin with this reference already exists

get.summary: List payins for a specific customer within Flow workflows
get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of payin transactions for a specific customer within
Flow workflows. This endpoint filters payins by the customer (merchant) and
supports filtering by client references and Flow workflow states.

parameters.name: customerDID
parameters.description: Customer DID identifier (merchant in payin context)

parameters.name: refs
parameters.description: Client references to filter by (single or array)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip for pagination

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Flow workflow states to filter by (single or array)

200.description: List of customer payins retrieved successfully

supportedAssets.description: List of supported assets for this transaction

get.summary: Get a specific payin for a customer within Flow workflows
get.description:
Retrieves detailed information about a specific payin transaction for a customer
within a Flow workflow context. This endpoint verifies that the payin belongs
to the specified customer and returns comprehensive transaction details.

parameters.name: customerDID
parameters.description: Customer DID identifier (merchant in payin context)

parameters.name: payinId
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the payin transaction

supportedAssets.description: List of supported assets for this transaction

400.description: Invalid request or transfer is not a payin

post.summary: Create a new payout for a customer within Flow workflows
post.description:
Creates a new payout transaction for a customer within a Flow workflow context.
This endpoint initiates a payout workflow where the customer can receive funds
by selecting a wallet and specifying where they want the funds sent.

parameters.name: customerDID
parameters.description: Customer DID identifier requesting the payout

supportedAssets.description: Optional list of supported assets for this transaction (CAIP-19)

lineTotal.description: Total price for this line item

400.description: Invalid request data or validation error

409.description: Payout with this reference already exists

get.summary: List payouts for a specific customer within Flow workflows
get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of payout transactions for a specific customer within
Flow workflows. This endpoint filters payouts by the customer and supports
filtering by client references and Flow workflow states.

parameters.name: customerDID
parameters.description: Customer DID identifier requesting the payouts

parameters.name: refs
parameters.description: Client references to filter by (single or array)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip for pagination

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Flow workflow states to filter by (single or array)

200.description: List of customer payouts retrieved successfully

supportedAssets.description: List of supported assets for this transaction

get.summary: Get a specific payout for a customer within Flow workflows
get.description:
Retrieves detailed information about a specific payout transaction for a customer
within a Flow workflow context. This endpoint verifies that the payout belongs
to the specified customer and returns comprehensive transaction details.

parameters.name: customerDID
parameters.description: Customer DID identifier requesting the payout

parameters.name: payoutId
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the payout transaction

supportedAssets.description: List of supported assets for this transaction

post.summary: Send signal to Flow payment workflow
post.description:
Sends customer signals to Flow payment workflows. This endpoint handles various
customer actions like wallet selection, payment approval/rejection, and payment viewing.
The workflow ID is extracted from the payment link data and signals are sent directly
to the appropriate Flow workflow.

parameters.name: paymentId
parameters.description: Payment ID extracted from payment link

action.description: The action to signal to the workflow

400.description: Invalid request or failed to process signal

404.description: Payment not found or not active

get.description:
Returns the latest authorization metadata (URL, issuer and expiry) associated with a
Flow transfer. If the responder has not published authorization details yet the endpoint
responds with HTTP 204.

parameters.name: paymentId
parameters.description: Transfer (payment) identifier

authorizationUrl.description: URL the initiator must visit to authorize the payment

from.description: DID of the party requesting authorization

expires.description: Expiration time for the authorization URL

404.description: Transfer not found for the entity

post.summary: Add agent to Flow payment workflow
post.description:
Adds an agent to a Flow payment workflow. This endpoint allows adding VASP agents
to handle specific aspects of the payment workflow, such as wallet providers or
compliance services.

parameters.name: paymentId
parameters.description: Payment ID for the workflow

for.description: Entity the agent is acting for

selectedAsset.description: Optional asset selected by customer from supported assets

500.description: Error while adding agent to flow

get.description:
Retrieves the current status of a Flow payment workflow. This includes the
workflow state, asset details, amounts, and associated agents.

parameters.name: paymentId
parameters.description: Payment ID for the workflow

post.summary: Set funding address for Flow transfer
post.description:
Allows onramps (agents in a Flow transfer) to provide funding address information.
The funding information is stored in the transfer (replacing any existing) and a Fund
TAP message is broadcast to all agents in the transfer. This endpoint should be called
after the onramp is added as an agent to the Flow transfer.

parameters.name: transferId
parameters.description: Transfer ID for the Flow transaction

amount.description: Amount to fund in the specified currency

fundingAccount.description: PayTo URI for fiat funding (RFC 8905). Supports iban, ach, bitcoin, void types.

for.description: DID of the party this funding is for (customerDID or merchantDID)

400.description: Invalid request or transfer is not a Flow transfer

403.description: Entity is not an agent in this transfer

get.summary: List payins for an entity with optional ref-based filtering
get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of payin transactions for an entity within Flow workflows.
Supports filtering by client references and Flow workflow states. Client references
are automatically converted to database references by adding the 'flow-' prefix.

parameters.name: refs
parameters.description: Client references to filter by (single or array)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip for pagination

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Flow workflow states to filter by (single or array)

parameters.name: is_initiator
parameters.description: Filter by initiator status (true for outgoing, false for incoming)

supportedAssets.description: List of supported assets for this transaction

post.summary: Create a new payin within Flow workflows (entity-level)
post.description:
Creates a new payin transaction within a Flow workflow context.
The payin includes invoice data, supported assets, fallback settlement addresses,
and generates a payment link for customer interaction.

supportedAssets.description: List of supported assets for this transaction (CAIP-19, USD, EUR)

fallbackSettlementAddresses.description: List of fallback settlement addresses (CAIP-10 or payto://)

lineTotal.description: Total price for this line item

400.description: Invalid request data or validation error

409.description: Payin with this reference already exists

get.summary: List incoming payins for an entity (as responder)
get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of incoming payin transactions where the entity acts as the responder
within Flow workflows. These are payins initiated by other entities that this entity needs to respond to.
Supports filtering by client references and Flow workflow states.

parameters.name: refs
parameters.description: Client references to filter by (single or array)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip for pagination

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Flow workflow states to filter by (single or array)

200.description: List of incoming payins retrieved successfully

supportedAssets.description: List of supported assets for this transaction

get.summary: List outgoing payins for an entity (as initiator)
get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of outgoing payin transactions where the entity acts as the initiator
within Flow workflows. These are payins initiated by this entity to other entities.
Supports filtering by client references and Flow workflow states.

parameters.name: refs
parameters.description: Client references to filter by (single or array)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip for pagination

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Flow workflow states to filter by (single or array)

200.description: List of outgoing payins retrieved successfully

supportedAssets.description: List of supported assets for this transaction

get.summary: Get a specific payin by ID (entity-level)
get.description:
Retrieves detailed information about a specific payin transaction within a Flow workflow.
This includes the payin status, asset details, amounts, and associated workflow data.

parameters.name: payinId
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the payin transaction

supportedAssets.description: List of supported assets for this transaction

post.summary: Authorize payin by confirming settlement address and optional asset
post.description:
Authorizes a payin by providing a settlement address and, optionally, the asset.
Requirements depend on whether the entity is initiator or responder in the workflow.

parameters.name: payinId
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the payin transaction

post.summary: Notify that web authorization is required for a payin
post.description:
Emits an AuthorizationRequired action for a PAYIN Flow transfer. The responding party
supplies a web `authorizationUrl` that the initiator must present to the customer for
confirmation before continuing the payment.

parameters.name: payinId
parameters.description: Payin transfer ID

authorizationUrl.description: Web URL the customer must visit to authorize the payin

get.summary: List payouts for an entity with optional ref-based filtering
get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of payout transactions for an entity within Flow workflows.
Supports filtering by client references and Flow workflow states. Client references
are automatically converted to database references by adding the 'flow-' prefix.

parameters.name: refs
parameters.description: Client references to filter by (single or array)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip for pagination

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Flow workflow states to filter by (single or array)

parameters.name: is_initiator
parameters.description: Filter by initiator status (true for outgoing, false for incoming)

supportedAssets.description: List of supported assets for this transaction

post.summary: Create a new payout within Flow workflows (entity-level)
post.description:
Creates a new payout transaction within a Flow workflow context.
This endpoint initiates a payout workflow where the merchant can receive funds
by selecting a wallet and specifying where they want the funds sent.

supportedAssets.description: Optional list of supported assets for this transaction (CAIP-19)

lineTotal.description: Total price for this line item

400.description: Invalid request data or validation error

409.description: Payout with this reference already exists

get.summary: List incoming payouts for an entity (as responder)
get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of incoming payout transactions where the entity acts as the responder
within Flow workflows. These are payouts initiated by other entities that this entity needs to respond to.
Supports filtering by client references and Flow workflow states.

parameters.name: refs
parameters.description: Client references to filter by (single or array)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip for pagination

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Flow workflow states to filter by (single or array)

200.description: List of incoming payouts retrieved successfully

supportedAssets.description: List of supported assets for this transaction

get.summary: List outgoing payouts for an entity (as initiator)
get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of outgoing payout transactions where the entity acts as the initiator
within Flow workflows. These are payouts initiated by this entity to other entities.
Supports filtering by client references and Flow workflow states.

parameters.name: refs
parameters.description: Client references to filter by (single or array)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip for pagination

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Flow workflow states to filter by (single or array)

200.description: List of outgoing payouts retrieved successfully

supportedAssets.description: List of supported assets for this transaction

get.summary: Get a specific payout by ID (entity-level)
get.description:
Retrieves detailed information about a specific payout transaction within a Flow workflow.
This includes the payout status, asset details, amounts, and associated workflow data.

parameters.name: payoutId
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the payout transaction

supportedAssets.description: List of supported assets for this transaction

post.summary: Authorize payout by selecting asset and optional settlement address
post.description:
Authorizes a payout by confirming the asset to be used and, optionally,
a settlement address. Requirements depend on whether the entity is initiator
or responder in the workflow.

parameters.name: payoutId
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the payout transaction

post.summary: Notify that web authorization is required for a payout
post.description:
Sends an AuthorizationRequired signal for a PAYOUT Flow transfer.
The responder provides a web `authorizationUrl` that the initiator should present to
the customer for approval. Optionally an `expires` ISO timestamp can be provided; if omitted,
a default TTL (1 hour) is applied by the server.

parameters.name: payoutId
parameters.description: Payout transfer ID

authorizationUrl.description: Web URL where the customer must complete authorization

expires.description: Optional ISO timestamp when the URL expires (defaults to 1 hour)

get.summary: List all relationships for an entity
get.description:
Retrieves a list of all relationships associated with the specified entity.
This includes relationship details and ownership proofs for each relationship.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Filter by the 'from' DID in the relationship

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Filter by the 'to' DID in the relationship

parameters.name: custodian
parameters.description: Filter by the custodian DID

post.summary: Create a new relationship
post.description: Creates a new relationship between an address and an entity. Relationships establish ownership or control over blockchain addresses. Optionally includes an ownership proof for verification.

patch.description: Confirms a relationship between an address and an entity. Creates the relationship if it doesn't exist (upsert behavior). Optionally includes an ownership proof for verification.

delete.description: Deletes an existing relationship between an address and an entity. Use 'from' and 'to' query parameters to identify the relationship.

schema.description: The source identifier of the relationship
The target identifier of the relationship

post.summary: Present PII data to counterparties
post.description:
Used to present PII data to counterparties in a transfer.
This endpoint is used for sharing PII between entities and can
optionally skip validation with skipValidation=true parameter.

get.description:
Returns the encryption key that would be sent in webhooks for the given entity.
This allows counterparties to proactively fetch encryption keys without waiting
for a RequirePresentation webhook.

**V1 VASP Handling:**
For entities using the TRNow protocol (V1 VASPs with did:ethr DIDs), this endpoint
automatically returns the Gateway VASP's encryption keys instead of the entity's own keys,
since V1 VASPs don't have their own encryption keys. The response includes a `gatewayDID`
field to indicate this.

**Performance Optimization:**
For did:web entities in the local database, encryption keys are resolved directly
without Network API calls, enabling fast response times for local V2 entities.

**Key Selection Priority:**
Uses the same priority algorithm as webhook sending:
1. Keys ending with "#pii" (highest priority)
2. Keys ending with "#notabene-pii"
3. Keys with type "X25519KeyAgreementKey2019"
4. First available key (fallback)

Response is cached via Veramo's DID resolution cache (5 minute TTL).

gatewayDID.description: Present only for V1 VASPs - indicates Gateway VASP DID used for encryption

404.description: Entity or encryption keys not found

value.message: No encryption keys found in DID document

message.example: Gateway VASP not configured for V1 protocol entities

gatewayNotConfigured.summary: Gateway not configured for V1 VASP

value.message: Gateway VASP not configured for V1 protocol entities
Could not select encryption key using priority algorithm

post.summary: Create a new transfer
post.description: Creates a new transfer from the specified entity to another entity. This is the entry point for initiating a new transfer in the system.

settlementId.description: Transaction hash in CAIP-220 format (e.g., eip155:1:tx/0x...). Raw hashes are automatically normalized to CAIP-220 when chain context is available from the asset.

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of transfers for the specified entity. Supports filtering by status, asset, direction, date range, and more.

post.summary: Create a new transfer (deprecated)
post.description:
**DEPRECATED**: Use `POST /entities/{entityDID}/tx` instead.

Creates a new transfer from the specified entity to another entity. This endpoint is maintained for backward compatibility.

settlementId.description: Transaction hash in CAIP-220 format (e.g., eip155:1:tx/0x...). Raw hashes are automatically normalized to CAIP-220 when chain context is available from the asset.

get.description:
**DEPRECATED**: Use `GET /entities/{entityDID}/tx` instead.

Retrieves a paginated list of transfers for the specified entity. This endpoint is maintained for backward compatibility.

post.summary: Authorize a transfer
post.description: Authorizes a transfer to proceed. Optionally accepts a settlement address and memo tag. Can be forced with the `force` query parameter to bypass certain checks, or use `ignoreFlags` to ignore flag policies.

post.summary: Authorize a transfer (deprecated)
post.description:
**DEPRECATED**: Use `POST /entities/{entityDID}/transfers/{transferId}/authorize` instead.

Authorizes a transfer to proceed. Optionally accepts a settlement address and memo tag. Can be forced with the `force` query parameter to bypass certain checks, or use `ignoreFlags` to ignore flag policies.

This endpoint is maintained for backward compatibility.

post.summary: Settle a transfer
post.description: Settles a transfer by providing settlement transaction details. This endpoint accepts optional settlement ID, settlement ID index, and revert settlement ID.

post.summary: Settle a transfer (deprecated)
post.description:
**DEPRECATED**: Use `POST /entities/{entityDID}/tx/{transferId}/settle` instead.

Settles a transfer by providing settlement transaction details. This endpoint is maintained for backward compatibility.

post.summary: Add an agent to a transfer
post.description: Adds an agent (VASP, custodian, etc.) to an existing transfer. The agent must have a valid DID and role.

post.summary: Add an agent to a transfer (deprecated)
post.description:
**DEPRECATED**: Use `POST /entities/{entityDID}/tx/{transferId}/agents` instead.

Adds an agent (VASP, custodian, etc.) to an existing transfer. This endpoint is maintained for backward compatibility.

get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific transfer including status, agents, and policy evaluations. Supports optional PII decryption, timeline, and integration results.

**DEPRECATED**: Use `GET /entities/{entityDID}/tx/{transferId}` instead.

Retrieves detailed information about a specific transfer including status, agents, and policy evaluations.
**DEPRECATED**: Use `GET /entities/{entityDID}/tx/search` instead.

schema.description: Sender address in CAIP-10 format (e.g., xrpl:0:rAddr:123456). Embedded memo tags are extracted and used for matching.

get.description: **DEPRECATED**: Use `GET /entities/{entityDID}/tx/match` instead.

schema.description: Sender address in CAIP-10 format (e.g., xrpl:0:rAddr:123456). Embedded memo tags are extracted and used for matching.

post.summary: Complete beneficiary name matching check
post.description: Marks the beneficiary name matching check as complete for the specified transfer. This endpoint is called after the entity has verified that the beneficiary name matches.

post.summary: Complete internal checks
post.description: Marks the internal checks as complete for the specified transfer. This endpoint is called after the entity has completed internal operational checks on the provided PII/Travel Rule information.

post.summary: Reject a transfer
post.description: Rejects a transfer with a specified reason. If the reason is OTHER, a comment is required.

reason.description: The reason for rejecting the transfer

comment.description: Additional comment (required when reason is OTHER)

post.summary: Reject a transfer (deprecated)
post.description:
**DEPRECATED**: Use `POST /entities/{entityDID}/tx/{transferId}/reject` instead.

Rejects a transfer with a specified reason. If the reason is OTHER, a comment is required.

reason.description: The reason for rejecting the transfer

comment.description: Additional comment (required when reason is OTHER)

post.summary: Replace an agent in a transfer
post.description: Replaces an existing agent in a transfer with a new agent. The caller must be the transfer initiator, a gateway, or have a relationship with the agent being replaced.

post.summary: Append PII data to a transfer
post.description: Appends IVMS101 PII (Personally Identifiable Information) data to an existing transfer. The PII data is validated against the transfer's presentation definition (if set) and encrypted before storage. All arrays in the IVMS101 structure are limited to 25 items to prevent unbounded payload attacks.

originator.description: Optional originator reference. When both originator and beneficiary @id are provided, enables PII reuse.

beneficiary.description: Optional beneficiary reference. When both originator and beneficiary @id are provided, enables PII reuse.

agents.description: Optional agents to add during append operation

examples: Transfer appended successfully with encrypted PII
message.default: Transfer appended successfully with encrypted PII

post.summary: Append PII data to a transfer (deprecated)
post.description:
**DEPRECATED**: Use `POST /entities/{entityDID}/tx/{transferId}/append` instead.

Appends IVMS101 PII (Personally Identifiable Information) data to an existing transfer. This endpoint is maintained for backward compatibility.

originator.description: Optional originator reference. When both originator and beneficiary @id are provided, enables PII reuse.

beneficiary.description: Optional beneficiary reference. When both originator and beneficiary @id are provided, enables PII reuse.

agents.description: Optional agents to add during append operation

examples: Transfer appended successfully with encrypted PII
message.default: Transfer appended successfully with encrypted PII

get.description: Searches the GLEIF registry for entity information using a Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) number.
Retrieves VASP/Entity information from the Network API by DID. Returns detailed information about the entity including regulatory status, verification status, and protocol support.

**DEPRECATED**: Use `GET /networks/{entityDID}` instead.

Retrieves VASP/Entity information from the Network API by DID. This endpoint is maintained for backward compatibility.

Returns a list of VASP entities in the network. Supports filtering by various criteria including sandbox mode, verification status, and search queries. Results are paginated and can be ordered by different fields.

post.summary: Create a new relationship (deprecated)
post.description:
**DEPRECATED**: Use `POST /entities/{entityDID}/relationships` instead.

Creates a new relationship between an address and an entity. This endpoint is maintained for backward compatibility.

patch.description:
**DEPRECATED**: Use `PATCH /entities/{entityDID}/relationships` instead.

Confirms a relationship between an address and an entity. This endpoint is maintained for backward compatibility.

delete.description:
**DEPRECATED**: Use `DELETE /entities/{entityDID}/relationships` with `from` and `to` query parameters instead.

Deletes an existing relationship between an address and an entity. This endpoint uses legacy `id` and `for` parameters for backward compatibility.

schema.description: The source identifier of the relationship (maps to 'from')
The target identifier of the relationship (maps to 'to')

get.description: Returns a paginated list of audit log events for an entity, with optional filters for domain, action, actor, and time range.

decryptParam.name: decrypt
decryptParam.description: Whether to decrypt PII data in the response

sanitizeParam.name: sanitize
sanitizeParam.description: Whether to sanitize sensitive data in the response

includeTimelineParam.name: include_timeline
includeTimelineParam.description: Whether to include the timeline of events for the transfer

includeIntegrationResultsParam.name: include_integration_results
includeIntegrationResultsParam.description: Whether to include integration results in the response

limitParam.name: limit
limitParam.description: Maximum number of transfers to return

createdBeforeParam.name: created_before
createdBeforeParam.description: Filter transfers created before this date

createdAfterParam.name: created_after
createdAfterParam.description: Filter transfers created after this date

actionRequiredParam.name: action_required
actionRequiredParam.description: Filter to only return transfers that require action (pending transfers awaiting response)

fieldsParam.name: fields
fieldsParam.description: Comma-separated list of fields to include in the response.

settlementAddressParam.name: settlement_address
settlementAddressParam.description: Settlement address, preferably in CAIP-10 format (e.g., eip155:1:0x...). CAIP-10 addresses get deep validation; plain addresses pass through as-is.

settlementIdIndexParam.name: settlement_id_index
settlementIdIndexParam.description: Optional index (vout/log index). Used for strict match; dropped in broadened search if strict fails.

created_beforeParam.name: created_before
created_beforeParam.description: Filter transfers created before this date (ISO 8601)

created_afterParam.name: created_after
created_afterParam.description: Filter transfers created after this date (ISO 8601)

request_for_informationParam.name: request_for_information
request_for_informationParam.description: Filter to transfers with pending information requests

include_replacedParam.name: include_replaced
include_replacedParam.description: Whether to include replaced agents in the response

forceParam.name: force
forceParam.description: Force the action even if checks fail

ignoreFlagsParam.name: ignoreFlags
ignoreFlagsParam.description: Comma-separated list of flags to ignore

BadRequest.description: Bad Request - The server could not process the request due to a client error.

Unauthorized.description: Unauthorized - Authentication information is missing or invalid.

example.error: Authentication token is missing or invalid

Forbidden.description: Forbidden - The server understood the request but refuses to authorize it.

example.error: You do not have permission to access this resource

NotFound.description: Not Found - The requested resource could not be found.

example.error: The requested resource could not be found

Conflict.description: Conflict - The request could not be completed due to a conflict with the current state of the resource.

example.error: A resource with this identifier already exists

InternalServerError.description: Internal Server Error - An unexpected condition was encountered on the server.

TransferDirection.description: The direction of a transfer relative to the entity

AssetIdentifier.description: Asset identifier in CAIP19 format, crypto symbol, or DTI format

DecimalAmount.description: Decimal amount as a string to maintain precision

SettlementIdentifier.description: Settlement transaction identifier in CAIP220 format

AgentRole.description: The role of the agent in the transfer

PolicyType.description: The type of policy to apply

error.description: Detailed error message explaining the conflict
error.example: Transfer with ref a7135b55-0822-4e96-86dc-3c43dfc6c333 already exists

IVMS101.description:
IVMS101 compliant data structure for originator and beneficiary information.
This structure is compliant with the official standard IVMS 101.2023.

**Geographic Address Structure - Use ONE of these patterns:**

**Pattern 1 (with addressLine):**
- addressType (required)
- addressLine (array of strings, required)
- townName (required)
- country (required - ISO-3166 Alpha-2 format, e.g., US, GB, XX)
- postCode (optional)

**Pattern 2 (with streetName/buildingNumber):**
- addressType (required)
- streetName (required if not using addressLine)
- buildingNumber (required if not using addressLine)
- townName (required)
- country (required - ISO-3166 Alpha-2 format, e.g., US, GB, XX)
- postCode (optional)

addressLine.description: Full address line(s) - use this OR streetName/buildingNumber

streetName.description: Street name - required if addressLine not provided

buildingNumber.description: Building number - required if addressLine not provided

country.description:
Two alphabetic characters representing an ISO-3166 Alpha-2 country code.
Includes the code 'XX' to represent 'an indicator for unknown States, other entities or organisations'.
Examples: US, GB, DE, FR, XX

countryOfIssue.description: Country that issued the identifier (optional, ISO-3166 Alpha-2)

dateOfBirth.description: Date of birth in YYYY-MM-DD format

legalPersonNameIdentifierType.description: Type of legal person name identifier

from.description: The DID of the source entity. Defaults to the entityDID from the path.

to.description: The DID of the target entity.

custodian.description: The DID of the custodian entity, if any.

proof.description: An ownership proof for the relationship.

memoTag.description: Optional memo tag or destination tag for address agents (SourceAddress, SettlementAddress)

ref.description: A unique reference for the transfer, used for idempotency.

accountNumber.description: The account number of the party

name.description: The name of the party (may be encrypted)

TapPolicy.description: TAP Policy in the format used by agent.policies

from.description: DID of the entity that originated the policy requirement

for.description: DID of the entity the policy is for

fromRole.description: Role of the entity that originated the policy requirement

fromAgent.description: Agent that originated the policy requirement

updatedAt.description: When the policy was last updated

presentationDefinition.description: URL or data for the presentation definition (for RequirePresentation policies)

purpose.description: The purpose of the policy request

about.description: Who the policy is about (e.g., originator, beneficiary)

aboutParty.description: The DID of the party this policy is about

aboutAgent.description: The DID of the agent this policy is about

nonce.description: A unique nonce for the policy request

encryptionKey.description: The encryption key of the counterparty for encrypting PII

reason.description: The reason for rejecting the transfer.

comment.description: Additional details about the rejection (required if reason is OTHER).

settlementId.description: The ID of the settlement transaction (e.g., transaction hash)

settlementIdIndex.description: The index of the settlement Id

proofs.description: Ownership proofs associated with this relationship

walletProvider.description: Wallet provider used for the proof

xpub.description: Extended public key (for HD wallet proofs)

url.description: URL to screenshot or external proof

confirmed.description: Whether the proof has been confirmed

proofs.description: Ownership proofs associated with this relationship

original.description: DID of the agent to be replaced
